sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INT, start_date DATE, interval_days INT);
SELECT id, unnest(ARRAY[DATE(start_date) - INTERVAL (d.interval_days) DAY]) AS adjusted_date FROM test_dates d WHERE d.interval_days IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, COUNT(unnest(ARRAY[DATE(start_date) - INTERVAL (d.interval_days) DAY])) AS num_adjusted_dates FROM test_dates d GROUP BY id HAVING num_adjusted_dates > 0;
SELECT id, unnest(CASE WHEN interval_days IS NOT NULL THEN ARRAY[DATE(start_date) - INTERVAL (d.interval_days) DAY] ELSE ARRAY[NULL] END) AS adjusted_date FROM test_dates d ORDER BY adjusted_date;
CREATE TABLE test_lists (list_id INT, days_list LIST<INT>);
INSERT INTO test_lists VALUES (1, [5, 12]), (2, [8, NULL]), (3, [0]), (4, NULL);
SELECT list_id, unnest(days_list) AS day FROM test_lists WHERE days_list IS NOT NULL;
SELECT list_id, DATE '2023-01-01' - INTERVAL (day) DAY AS past_date FROM test_lists t JOIN unnest(t.days_list) AS day ON t.list_id = t.list_id WHERE day IS NOT NULL;
SELECT list_id, COUNT(unnest(days_list)) AS item_count FROM test_lists WHERE days_list IS NOT NULL GROUP BY list_id;

SELECT list_id, AVG(DATE '2023-01-01' - INTERVAL (day) DAY) AS avg_past_date FROM test_lists t JOIN unnest(t.days_list) AS day ON t.list_id = t.list_id GROUP BY list_id HAVING AVG(DATE '2023-01-01' - INTERVAL (day) DAY) IS NOT NULL;
